original_hypothesis.title: confirmEdgeByTime Assertion Blocks Underflow if Second Child Removed
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: In confirmEdgeByTime, when the edge is block-level and isFirstChild, assertionBlocks is calculated as getSecondChildCreationBlock(prevHash) - getFirstChildCreationBlock(prevHash). The edge creation check verifies hasSibling at creation time, but if the predecessor's second child data could somehow become 0 between edge creation and confirmation, the subtraction would revert due to arithmetic underflow (Solidity 0.8). While this should be impossible (childCreated only sets these values, never clears them), there is no explicit guard at the point of subtraction.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The assertion child creation data is immutable after setting (childCreated never resets values). However, the lack of a defensive check at the subtraction point means any future code path that could clear secondChildBlock would cause a DoS on edge confirmation.

proof_of_signal.pattern: assertionBlocks = getSecondChildCreationBlock(prev) - getFirstChildCreationBlock(prev); no explicit check that second > first or second > 0

preconditions: Block-level edge claiming a first-child assertion
Predecessor assertion's secondChildBlock somehow becomes 0 or less than firstChildBlock
This condition should be unreachable in current code

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: Unchecked subtraction relying on external contract state invariant
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: If secondChildBlock == 0, subtraction underflows and reverts; DoS on honest edge confirmation
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No require(secondChildBlock > 0) or require(secondChildBlock >= firstChildBlock) at point of use

analysis_questions: Can any admin function or upgrade path reset secondChildBlock to 0?
Are there any code paths in RollupCore that clear assertion child data?
If the assertion chain implementation changes, could this invariant be broken?
validation_requirements: Verify childCreated never resets values in any code path
Confirm no admin function can modify assertion child block values
Test edge confirmation with various assertion predecessor states

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Currently unreachable. If a future upgrade or admin action could reset secondChildBlock, honest validators would be unable to confirm their edges via time, permanently stalling the rollup challenge resolution.
prerequisites: Requires secondChildBlock to be 0 despite hasSibling being true at edge creation

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: DoS on honest edge confirmation if invariant is broken by future changes
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All validators attempting to confirm edges by time

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No defensive test for edge case where assertion child data is inconsistent

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
